Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This removes focus indicators for keyboard users, making it impossible to determine which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 258: Add a visible focus style, such as outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); or box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the recommended 24px minimum touch target size. This can make the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 448: Increase min-height to at least 24px, or ensure sufficient padding to achieve a 44px touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the recommended 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 458: Increase min-width and min-height to at least 24px, or ensure sufficient padding to achieve a 44px touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input has a height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target. This can make the input difficult to tap on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 550: Increase the height to at least 44px, or adjust padding and line-height to increase the touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width and height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 570: Increase the width and height to at least 44px, or adjust padding to increase the touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has min-height and min-width of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 512: Increase the min-height and min-width to at least 44px, or adjust padding to increase the touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: These navigation links and buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 530: Increase the min-height to at least 44px, or adjust padding to increase the touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu links have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 545: Increase the min-height to at least 44px, or adjust padding to increase the touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 764: Increase the min-height to at least 44px, or adjust padding to increase the touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: Increase the min-height to at least 44px, or adjust padding to increase the touch target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class has a min-height of 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target. While it may be larger than 24px, it still may be difficult for some users to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: Increase the min-height to 44px, or adjust padding to achieve a 44px touch target.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class does not hide content visually. It uses position: static and clip: auto, which leaves the content visible on screen. This fails to hide content from sighted users while keeping it available for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 276: Replace the .sr-only rule with: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform and transition on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for it is empty. This means the motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 650: Add rules inside the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable the motion, e.g., .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text more difficult to read, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 183: Remove the negative word-spacing or set it to a non-negative value.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links, relying solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text. This can be problematic for users with colour vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 197: Add an alternative visual indicator, such as underlining on hover, or set a default underline for links.

fpTitles: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open(), mobileNav.close(), and mobileNav.toggle() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2023: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2029: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`. Line 2035: update the toggle function to set the attribute based on the new state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on dropdown menu trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions change the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on their trigger buttons. Screen reader users will not know if dropdowns are open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2123: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2133: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed for billing period selection
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function updates visual active state on billing period buttons but does not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3018: replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers for product filtering
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product count and grid but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2409: after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : \`${totalMatches} products found\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields as invalid visually and with error messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen reader users may not be alerted to the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3289: in the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`. In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` after `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the page loads. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2070: after `_initialised = true;` add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for dropdown menu triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons when they are registered. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions of the mobile navigation do not set the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed when the navigation menu is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.open function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In the mobileNav.close function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open and close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the expanded state of dropdown menus, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In _closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode toggle does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The grid/list view toggle buttons do not set aria-pressed to indicate the active view mode. Screen reader users cannot tell which layout is active, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _onViewToggle, after setting _productFilterState.viewMode, update all view-toggle buttons: setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode));

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count and visible cards are not announced to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods (monthly/annual) updates prices but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology may miss the update.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod, after updating the prices, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is invalid, the code adds a CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification).
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); when valid, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an initial aria-expanded value when the page loads. Screen reader users may not know the initial state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after setting _trigger, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not given an initial aria-expanded="false" when registered. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register, after setting aria-controls, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch has role="switch" but does not update aria-checked when toggled. Screen reader users cannot perceive the current state (month/year) of the switch.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, add: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year');

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as a radio group but do not convey the active state to screen readers. The active tab is only indicated visually with a CSS class.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncTabAriaStates function, add: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive);

issuesFound.title: Dynamic filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count and visible cards change dynamically without an ARIA live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed of the results.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Dynamic price updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates all price displays without announcing the change to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology won't know prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateSavingsMessage, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Invalid form fields are styled and described but aria-invalid is not set. Screen readers may not announce the error state without this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); when valid, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are closed on page load but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false". Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the register function, after panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''), add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button's expanded state is not initialized with aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot know the menu's initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function, after _trigger is assigned, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown button that toggles a submenu has aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. This prevents users of assistive technology from knowing if the submenu is currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1004: <button ... aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>

issuesFound.title: Icon-only interactive element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle IconButton has no aria-label prop, making it inaccessible to screen reader users. Icon-only buttons must have an accessible name to convey their purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1258: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} ...>

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The icon inside ThemeToggle is purely decorative but lacks aria-hidden, causing screen readers to potentially announce the emoji character. This adds unnecessary verbosity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1259: {ICONS[theme]}

issuesFound.title: Interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The BillingToggle buttons indicate selection visually but lack aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot tell which billing period is currently active.

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility broken (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static; width: auto; height: auto; clip: auto;` which does not hide content from visual users while keeping it accessible. This defeats its purpose for screen‑reader‑only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0,0,0,0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative (global a)
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no alternative visual cue (color change, border, background) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on visual differentiation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`  /* or add a distinct color/border on hover/focus */

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override (.btn)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` includes `transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast);` which animates transforms, but there is no reduced‑motion media query overriding this transition, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add inside a reduced‑motion block: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override (.hero-ctas .btn-primary)
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary` transitions `transform` on hover, but no reduced‑motion override is provided, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users preferring reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override (.pricing-card--featured:hover)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `.pricing-card--featured:hover` applies a `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01);` with a transition, yet no reduced‑motion media query disables this animation.
issuesFound.fix: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The element selected by `SELECTORS.navToggle` (the hamburger button) is used to open and close the mobile navigation drawer, but the script never sets or updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute to reflect the drawer’s state. This prevents screen‑reader users from knowing whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In the `open()` function set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` and in the `close()` function set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Also add `aria-controls` pointing to the drawer’s ID when initializing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The same toggle button does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that references the navigation drawer element, so assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining `_drawer` in `mobileNav.init()`, ensure the drawer has an `id` (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav-drawer')`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle switch missing aria‑checked state
issuesFound.explanation: The element selected by `qs('[data-billing-toggle]')` acts as an on/off switch for monthly/annual billing, but the script never updates an ARIA state (`aria‑checked` or `aria‑pressed`) to indicate its current position. This violates WCAG 2.1 (ARIA 1.1) for toggle switches.
issuesFound.fix: In the click handler `_onToggleClick()` (and any keyboard handler), after toggling the period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'))`. Also ensure the element has `role="switch"` (already set) and an initial `aria-checked` value on load.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set `aria-invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module validates fields on blur/input and displays error messages, but its `_renderResult` function only adds CSS classes and error text; it never sets the `aria-invalid` attribute on the field when invalid, nor clears it when valid. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `result.valid` is false, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, remove the attribute or set it to `'false'`. Ensure this runs before adding/removing CSS classes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown registration adds aria‑haspopup and aria‑controls to the trigger button, but never sets aria‑expanded to reflect the closed state on page load. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In the register function, after setting aria‑haspopup and aria‑controls, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` so the trigger is correctly marked as collapsed initially.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are parsed, the code sets aria‑controls and aria‑labelledby but does not set aria‑expanded on the question button. Without this attribute, screen‑reader users cannot know the default collapsed state of each FAQ item.
issuesFound.fix: In faqRegistry.parse after creating trigger and panel, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` so each FAQ trigger is explicitly marked as collapsed on page load.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector has a hover animation (`transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg)`) but lacks a `prefers-reduced-motion` override to disable or reduce this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 920: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links, relying solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or provide an alternative visual indicator (e.g., `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }`)

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can impair readability, especially for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`, which can impair readability, especially for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: `body { letter-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` selector sets `font-size: 12px`, which is below the recommended minimum of 16px for body text, reducing readability for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: `html { font-size: 16px; }`

fpTitles: Font size below 16px for body text

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` removes the default focus outline without providing a visible alternative for keyboard users who rely on focus indicators to navigate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` selector sets a `min-height` and `min-width` of `28px`, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 px (recommended 44×44 px). This can make it difficult for users with motor impairments to interact with the element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1106: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` selector applies a `transform` and `box-shadow` transition on hover, which involves motion. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or reduce this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1326: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links, and there is no alternative visual indicator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom, or background highlight on hover) to distinguish links from surrounding text. This can make it difficult for users with low vision or colour blindness to identify links.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. The correct implementation should use `position: absolute`, `clip`, and small dimensions to hide content visually without removing it from the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or visual impairments by making text harder to parse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle element (navToggle) is a `<div>` or `<span>` styled as a button but lacks keyboard event handlers for Enter/Space. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot open/close the mobile menu.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle function `toggle()` does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger element. Screen readers cannot announce the open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close state change is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers are unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation function `validateField()` does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. Screen readers cannot announce the error state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation function does not clear `aria-invalid` when a field becomes valid. Screen readers continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger element does not have `aria-expanded="false"` set on page load. Screen readers cannot announce the initial closed state.

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for non-native interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onPointerDown` and `_onPointerUp` handlers for swipe detection on the testimonials carousel are attached to pointer events but lack corresponding keyboard handlers (Enter/Space) to ensure the carousel is operable via keyboard. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger swipe actions.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen readers will not announce the state change, leaving keyboard users unaware of the menu's open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4823: Inside the `open` function, add:
```
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
```
    Line 4839: Inside the `close` function, add:
```
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateSavingsMessage` function in `pricing` updates the text content of the savings message element (`_savingsMsg`) but does not announce this change to screen readers via an ARIA live region. Users who rely on screen readers will not be aware of the updated savings information when switching billing periods.

fpTitles: Missing keyboard handler for non-native interactive element

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle (hamburger button) has pointerdown and pointerup event listeners for swipe detection, but lacks corresponding keydown (Enter/Space) handlers. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot activate the swipe-to-close functionality.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the mobile navigation module toggles the visibility of the drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the state change.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation module emits custom events (`mobilenav:open`, `mobilenav:close`) but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers will not know the navigation menu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation module initialises the mobile menu but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button at page load. Screen readers may not correctly announce the initial closed state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in the inline validator module validates fields but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. Screen readers will not announce the error state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in the inline validator module does not clear `aria-invalid="false"` on valid fields. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in the pricing module toggles the billing period but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button. Screen readers will not announce the state change.

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon in the NavDropdown component is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the arrow characters, which is redundant since the button's accessible name already conveys the state via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1502: <span aria-hidden="true" className="nav-link__chevron">

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required-field marker (asterisk) in FormGroup is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the asterisk as text, which is redundant since the "required" state is already conveyed via the VisuallyHidden "(required)" text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 850: *

issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component in FormGroup is not connected to the input via aria-describedby. Screen readers will not announce the error message when the input receives focus, preventing users from understanding the error.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle CSS classes and scroll lock but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation menu is currently open or closed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function fails to set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state where screen readers may report the item as collapsed even when it is visually expanded.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field has an error immediately upon focus.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute. This leaves the field in a permanently invalid state from the perspective of assistive technology, even after the user has corrected the error.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count update is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._updateCountBadge function updates the text content of the badge when filters are applied, but this change is not announced via a live region. Users relying on screen readers are not informed that the number of visible products has changed.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input does not update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module implements a combobox pattern (role="combobox", aria-haspopup="listbox") but the _open and _close functions do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the input element. Screen readers will not indicate whether the suggestion list is currently visible.

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top button click action is not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page to the top and moves focus, but it does not announce this action to screen readers. While the keyboard shortcut version announces the action, the button click itself leaves screen reader users unaware of the context change.

issuesFound.title: Pricing card updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._updatePlanCard function updates the text content of price elements when the billing period changes. This dynamic update is not pushed to a live region, so screen reader users are not informed that the price has changed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu state change.

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only utility class makes content visible instead of hiding it
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which keeps content visible in the layout rather than visually hiding it while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility class and exposes screen-reader-only text to sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 503-514: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Search form label removed from accessibility tree with display:none
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search label.sr-only applies display: none, which removes the label from the accessibility tree entirely. Screen reader users will not hear the label for the search input, making the form control inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 621: #site-search label.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Global focus-visible outline removal without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The universal :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border-color. issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px. While above the 24px minimum, this is below the 44px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.5 Target Size Enhanced), and with only 6px padding (space-2), the total touch area remains below 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 588: min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; (or increase padding to achieve 44px total)

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu items touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The selectors #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button set min-height: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size, making navigation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 605: min-height: 44px; (or ensure total clickable area including padding reaches 44px)

issuesFound.title: Search input touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input selector sets height: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size, making the search field difficult to tap accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 632: height: 44px; (or min-height: 44px)

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 649-650: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 834: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 920: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Button component transform transition lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 534), creating spatial movement on state changes. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who experience vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 526-534: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } } or specifically remove transform from transition in reduced motion mode.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transition: transform var(--transition-slower), creating a 3D perspective animation on hover. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 724 is empty and does not disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 724-727: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The selector nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a sets text-decoration: none (inherited from global a rule at line 261) and provides only color change on hover (line 647) without underline, border-bottom, or other non-colour visual differentiator. Links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 647: text-decoration: underline; (or add border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colour-brand-primary))

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets text-decoration: none and provides only color change on hover (line 1043) without underline or other non-colour visual differentiator. Links are distinguished by colour alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1043: text-decoration: underline; (or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide the dropdown panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1570: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
    Line 1580: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions toggle the drawer visibility and CSS classes but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1669: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`
    Line 1680: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (_syncToggleState) function as toggle buttons indicating the active billing cycle but do not set aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2567: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils form validation missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Conversely, markValid removes describedby but fails to clear aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1541: Add `control.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(control, errorEl);`
    Line 1554: Add `control.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `control.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`) after updating the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are initialised in the register function, the trigger button lacks aria-expanded="false" for menus that start in a closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1599: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the menu starts in a closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1718: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but the _open and _close functions do not update aria-expanded to reflect whether the suggestion listbox is displayed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2393: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`
    Line 2400: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion list is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2343: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after mounting the listbox.

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count and results are not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2454: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products match your filters`);` (or similar appropriate message).

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/annual), the price updates are not announced to screen readers, so users may not realize the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2543: After updating the plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${period} billing`);` (or use STRINGS.pricingMonthly/pricingAnnual).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation menu open and close actions are not announced via the live region utility, so screen reader users are not notified when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1674: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` after opening the menu.
    Line 1693: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` after closing the menu.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens but does not trap focus within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab outside the drawer to background elements, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1670: After opening, add `const trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap = trap; trap.activate();`
    Line 1680: Before closing, add `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus move on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not moved from the trigger button into the drawer, forcing keyboard users to tab through the entire page to reach the new content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1670: After opening, add `const first = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (first) first.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed (via Escape or outside click), focus is not returned to the trigger button even though the returnFocus parameter is accepted by _closeInstance. Keyboard users must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1560: In the _closeInstance function body, add `if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus();` after `inst.open = false;` (or at the end of the function).

fpTitles: mobileNav missing focus move on open
dropdownMenu missing focus return on close

issuesFound.title: sr-only utility class does not visually hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers, but the CSS properties (position: static, width: auto, overflow: visible, clip: auto) make content fully visible instead. This breaks the screen-reader-only pattern and will cause visually hidden elements like skip links, hidden headings, and form labels to appear on screen when they should not.
issuesFound.fix: Line 338-347: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: :focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing an alternative visible focus indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard-only users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 318-321: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: :focus-visible in forced-colors mode removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Within the @media (forced-colors: active) block, :focus-visible sets outline: none without providing any visible focus indicator. High contrast mode users will have no visual indication of focused elements, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 328-332: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid Highlight; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has letter-spacing: -0.05em which tightens character spacing globally. Negative letter-spacing reduces readability for users with low vision, cognitive disabilities, or dyslexia, and can make text appear cramped.

issuesFound.title: Links have no underline — colour alone distinguishes links
issuesFound.explanation: The rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links globally.
