issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Filter toggle buttons have outline: none on :focus-visible with no visible replacement, preventing keyboard users from seeing which filter is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 818-820: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Billing toggle buttons (Monthly/Annual) have no visible focus indicator after outline removal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 934-936: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on table wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper (which may receive keyboard focus) has no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1048-1050: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on FAQ accordion buttons
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ expand/collapse buttons have outline: none with no visible replacement, leaving keyboard users unable to track focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: Form checkboxes have no visible focus indicator after outline removal, making it impossible for keyboard users to see focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1195: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand section links have no visible focus indicator after outline removal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1215-1217: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have outline: none with no visible replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220-1223: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer have no visible focus indicator after outline removal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1228-1231: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors (high contrast) mode
issuesFound.explanation: In Windows High Contrast Mode, the :focus-visible outline is removed without any replacement. Users relying on forced colors will have no visible focus indicator anywhere on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 258-262: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid Highlight; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon-only buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments will have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 401-405: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes are sized at 20×20 pixels, below the 24×24 pixel minimum target size. Users with motor impairments will have difficulty selecting checkboxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1184: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em which reduces spacing between words by 20%. This can cause words to appear merged, reducing readability for users with cognitive disabilities, low vision, or reading difficulties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 159: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; from the body rule, or change to word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) default. This makes all text smaller than browser defaults, reducing readability for users who have not set a custom default size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 144: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 1rem; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Line-height below recommended minimum on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph elements have line-height: 1, which is significantly below the recommended 1.5 minimum for body text. This tight line spacing impairs readability, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: p { line-height: var(--line-height-normal); text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links. For links in body text/prose that rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text, this violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Links must have a non-color visual differentiator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 191: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; } and add a contextual rule: .prose a { text-decoration: underline; } (or ensure all links have background/border/font-weight differentiation)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, meaning the translateY(1px) on :active will animate. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation. WCAG 2.3.3 requires motion from interactions to be suppressible.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 416: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition and 3D rotation effect, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 722-727 is empty and does not disable the transform. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D rotation animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 722-727: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover rule applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), but this is not covered by the reduced-motion override for .pricing-card:hover because .pricing-card--featured is a more specific selector. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 984: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on FAQ accordion buttons
Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on footer navigation links
Line-height below recommended minimum on paragraphs
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not set aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function hides the panel and emits an event but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2991: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `if (!inst.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the panel starts hidden but the trigger's aria-expanded attribute is never initialized. Screen readers have no initial state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3068: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function sets item.open to true and shows the panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5256: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function toggles CSS.active class on period buttons but never sets aria-pressed. Toggle buttons require aria-pressed to communicate their state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5755: add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing switch role element missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element is assigned role="switch" but aria-checked is never set. Switch widgets require aria-checked to communicate their on/off state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5820: add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));` inside the init function after setting role="switch".

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles CSS.active class on filter buttons but never sets aria-pressed. Filter toggle buttons require aria-pressed to communicate their pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4704: add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message but never sets aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1942: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message from aria-describedby but never clears aria-invalid. The field remains marked as invalid even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1951: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after the aria-describedby cleanup block.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and products are shown/hidden, the count badge is updated visually but no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the result count. Users cannot discover how many products match their filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4585: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(activeCategory) || 'Showing products');` inside the animation callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the trigger element receives aria-haspopup and aria-controls but not aria-expanded="false". The initial collapsed state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2881: After `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`, add `if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen reader users are not notified that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2413: After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message link but never clears aria-invalid from the field. Screen reader users may continue to hear that the field is invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2429: After the aria-describedby cleanup, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter._updateCountBadge() does not announce count change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product count badge updates after filtering, the change is not announced to screen reader users. The count badge element does not have an associated live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3872: After `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count === 0 ? 'all' : count) || \`Showing \${count} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing price display changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes and prices update, screen reader users are not notified of the new prices. The price change is purely visual with no live region announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4920: After `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not move focus into the navigation drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the trigger button rather than moving to the first focusable element inside the drawer. Keyboard users must manually Tab into the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2680: After `_lockScroll();`, add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not restore focus to the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the focus order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

fpTitles: mobileNav.open() does not move focus into the navigation drawer
mobileNav.close() does not restore focus to the trigger button

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialisation does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The initial state is ambiguous for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2881: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not manage keyboard focus when opening/closing
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved into the drawer. When it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger. Keyboard users may be stranded without a visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2802: add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` after `_lockScroll();` and Line 2820: add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` after `_unlockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open function removes the hidden attribute and adds a CSS class, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2958: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not set aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown close function adds the hidden attribute and removes the CSS class, but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2965: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function sets item.open to true and animates the panel, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4190: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function links the field to the error message via aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2445: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="false" or removes the attribute. Screen reader users may still hear the field announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2457: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after the closing brace of the if block.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter changes
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible count badge and empty state message change, but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the filtering result.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3835: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);` after `_updateEmptyState(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggle changes, prices update visually but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4527: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));` after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`

fpTitles: mobileNav does not manage keyboard focus when opening/closing
faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop (per its TypeScript interface), but ThemeToggle passes only children (emoji icons) without an aria-label. Screen readers will announce only the emoji or nothing meaningful, failing WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 860: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} aria-label={LABELS[theme]} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label not programmatically associated with input
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an htmlFor prop but never applies it to the <label> element. Without htmlFor, the label is not programmatically associated with its input, so screen reader users cannot click the label to focus the input and may not hear the label announced when entering the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 630: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal placed on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: aria-modal="true" is placed on the modal-overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog" (modal-dialog). Screen readers may not correctly convey the dialog's modal nature.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1930: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Fix: Line 1932: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop and applies an error CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users are not notified when a field has a validation error, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 660: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop and applies an error CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users are not notified when a field has a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 675: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop and applies an error CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users are not notified when a field has a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 700: <select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The btn-spinner span rendered during loading has no aria-hidden="true". While a visually hidden loading label is provided, the decorative spinner element should be explicitly hidden from assistive technology to prevent potential confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The star characters (★ and ☆) are rendered inside a span that has no aria-hidden. The outer span has an aria-label, but the inner decorative content may still be announced by some screen readers, causing redundant output.
issuesFound.fix: Line 550: {stars.map((filled, i) => ({filled ? '★' : '☆'}))}

issuesFound.title: StatItem formatted values not screen-reader-friendly
issuesFound.explanation: The Stat interface defines valueLabel for screen-reader-friendly pronunciation (e.g., "40,000+" → "40,000 plus"), but the component renders only value. Screen readers may mispronounce formatted values like "4.2M" or "99.9%".
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1075:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: Section component ignores labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component receives a labelledby prop intended to provide aria-labelledby for landmark regions, but never applies it to the rendered element. Sections using this prop will not have proper accessible names.
issuesFound.fix: Line 695: <Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: ComparisonCell decorative symbols should be aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The checkmark (✓) and em-dash (—) symbols have aria-label for screen readers, but the visual symbols themselves are not hidden. Some screen readers may announce both the aria-label and the symbol character, causing redundant output.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1325: {value} Fix: Line 1329: {value}

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop for accessibility, but ThemeToggle does not pass one. The LABELS object is defined with appropriate labels but never used. Screen reader users will hear no accessible name for this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1020: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for closing the mobile navigation panel does not have an aria-label prop. The IconButton interface requires aria-label for icon-only buttons. Screen reader users will not hear an accessible name for this close button.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~950: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for navigating to the previous testimonial does not have an aria-label prop. Screen reader users will not hear an accessible name identifying this button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1485: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for navigating to the next testimonial does not have an aria-label prop. Screen reader users will not hear an accessible name identifying this button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1490: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not pass aria-invalid to the underlying input element. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine when a field has a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~715: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not pass aria-invalid to the underlying textarea element. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine when a field has a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~735: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not pass aria-invalid to the underlying select element. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine when a field has a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~760: <select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div but should be on the element with role="dialog". Screen readers may not correctly identify the dialog as modal, potentially allowing focus to escape to background content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1735: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Fix: Line ~1740: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav navigation region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element inside MobileNav does not have an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. When multiple navigation regions exist on a page, each should have a unique accessible name to help screen reader users distinguish between them.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~975: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element in SkipLinks does not have an aria-label attribute. While skip links are typically hidden, the navigation region should still have an accessible name for proper landmark identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~540: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element has an id attribute but no aria-label. When multiple navigation regions exist on a page, each should have a unique accessible name to help screen reader users distinguish between them.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~920: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the button should have aria-busy="true" to communicate the loading state to screen reader users. Currently only aria-disabled is set, which doesn't convey that an operation is in progress.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~620: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Spinner decorative ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner__ring span is a decorative visual element that should be hidden from assistive technology. The parent element already provides the accessible name via aria-label and VisuallyHidden, so the ring should have aria-hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~650: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The inner span containing the star characters (★ and ☆) is decorative since the outer span already provides the accessible name via aria-label. The star characters should be hidden from screen readers to prevent redundant or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~555: {stars.map((filled, i) => ({filled ? '★' : '☆'}))}

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron span containing the up/down arrow characters (▲ and ▼) is decorative and should be hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce these characters literally, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~890: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links with item.current set to true receive a CSS class indicating the current page but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~925: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links with item.current set to true receive a CSS class indicating the current page but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~985: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: StatItem value missing accessible name for formatted numbers
issuesFound.explanation: The stat.value contains formatted strings like "40,000+" which may be announced confusingly by screen readers. The stat.valueLabel property provides a clearer pronunciation ("40,000 plus") but is not used. Screen reader users may not understand the formatted value correctly.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1130:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: Required field asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk character (*) in the required field marker is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce "asterisk" literally, which is confusing. The VisuallyHidden component already provides "(required)" text, so the asterisk should be aria-hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~680: *

fpTitles: DesktopNav navigation region missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton with only an icon (emoji) as content, but does not pass the required aria-label prop. Screen reader users will hear no accessible name for this button, making its purpose completely unclear.
issuesFound.fix: Line 918: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual toggle buttons visually indicate which billing period is selected via styling, but lack aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1274: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>`
  Fix: Line 1281: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons visually indicate which category is active via styling, but lack aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1113: `<button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon (▲/▼) inside the dropdown trigger button is decorative — the button already has the label text. Without aria-hidden, screen readers may announce the arrow character redundantly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 745: `{isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}`

issuesFound.title: Required field asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk visual marker for required fields is announced by screen readers as "asterisk" or "*", creating redundant or confusing output. The accompanying VisuallyHidden "(required)" already provides the accessible indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 617: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a button is in the loading state, the spinner span is rendered without aria-hidden. Although a VisuallyHidden loading label provides the accessible announcement, the spinner element itself may still be exposed to some assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: StarRating star characters missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The star characters (★/☆) inside the rating component are decorative — the outer span already has an aria-label providing the full rating. Without aria-hidden, screen readers may announce the star characters redundantly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: `{filled ? '★' : '☆'}`

issuesFound.title: Trust badge images should be decorative
issuesFound.explanation: Each trust badge contains both an image with alt text and visible text content (e.g., "WCAG 2.2 AA"). Screen readers will announce both the image alt text and the visible text, creating redundant output. The images should be marked decorative since the text already conveys the information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1009: `WCAG 2.2 AA` Fix: Line 1012: `SOC 2 Type II` Fix: Line 1015: `GDPR Ready`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal overlay div, but it should be on the dialog element itself (the element with role="dialog"). This may cause screen readers to incorrectly interpret the modal's boundaries.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1796: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>`
  Fix: Line 1802: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`

issuesFound.title: StatItem value missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The stat value (e.g., "40,000+") may be announced confusingly by screen readers. The Stat interface includes a valueLabel property ("40,000 plus") for screen reader friendly pronunciation, but it is not applied to the element.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1052: `

{stat.value}

`

issuesFound.title: Section component ignores labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop intended to provide an accessible name via aria-labelledby, but the prop is never applied to the rendered element. Sections using this prop will not have their accessible name set.
issuesFound.fix: Line 652: `<Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm success message emoji not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The success message contains an emoji (🎉) that may be announced inconsistently by screen readers. Emojis should be hidden from assistive technology when they are purely decorative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: `🎉 You're subscribed! Check your inbox for a confirmation email.`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm success message emoji not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The success message contains an emoji (✅) that may be announced inconsistently by screen readers. Emojis should be hidden from assistive technology when they are purely decorative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1770: `✅ Message received! We'll be in touch soon.`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links with item.current=true receive a visual indicator (nav-link--current class) but lack aria-current="page" to communicate the current page state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 785: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links with item.current=true receive a visual indicator (mobile-nav__link--current class) but lack aria-current="page" to communicate the current page state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 855: `<a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

fpTitles: Trust badge images should be decorative
